name: Steamed Cranberry Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease two 1 quart molds and sprinkle with granulated sugar.
2. Combine the cranberries, walnuts, and apricots and dredge with 1/4 cup of the flour.
3. Sift the remaining flour, baking powder, and salt together. Add the bread crumbs.
4. Beat together the brown sugar, suet, and eggs. Mix in the dry ingredients alternately with the milk to the suet mixture.
5. Stir in the almond extract and the floured fruit and walnuts, combining well.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared molds. The molds should be no more than three-quarters full.
7. Cover with a double thickness of wax paper and foil and tie securely with string.
8. Place on a rack in a large pot. Pour boiling water around the molds to two-thirds of the way up the molds.
9. Cover tightly and steam for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until the top of the pudding is no longer sticky. Add more boiling water if necessary during this time, but ensure the water just simmers in the pan as the pudding steams.
10. Remove the molds from the water. Let the pudding set in the molds for about 5 minutes before turning out onto a warm platter and serving hot with Caramel Sauce, Almond Sauce, or Hard Sauce. If using Hard Sauce, try substituting 2 tablespoons of apricot brandy for the flavoring.
11. Yield: 12 servings.
